summary: Comprehensive e2e testing of Budget Tracker landing page + backend lead capture. All 9 backend pytest tests passed (root, status, leads POST/GET/validation/limits). All 14 frontend feature checks passed: every section renders, Hero/Pricing CTAs link to correct external Stripe checkout URLs with target=_blank, nav anchors point to #features/#calculator/#pricing/#faq, all 4 InteractiveDemo tabs update detail panel, ROI sliders (spend + drift) update savings output in real time, FAQ accordion opens, lead form submits successfully and shows the 'You're on the list' success state, 0 console errors/warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend server.py uses @app.on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI; prefer lifespan handler (low priority - still functional).
Lead form 'required field validation' currently relies on HTML5 `required` attribute + toast on submit; native browser tooltip prevents submission when name/email empty. Functional and acceptable.
CORS_ORIGINS defaults to '*' which is fine for a public marketing site, but consider restricting in production deployment.
Backend file server.py is well-scoped (~125 lines) - clean. Frontend landing components are modular and under size thresholds.
seed_data_creation: Test leads created in MongoDB with TEST_ prefix and unique uuid emails (test_*@example.com, min_*@example.com, playwright_*@test.com). Safe to ignore or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend API base: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL/api. New endpoints are POST /api/leads (201) and GET /api/leads. Frontend uses TID constants from src/lib/testIds.js for every interactive element. External Stripe checkout URLs are intentional (budget.konticode.com/checkout.php?plan=starter|growth|scale and demo-slideshow.php) — do NOT treat as broken navigation. Lead form success state replaces form with 'You're on the list' panel + Growth CTA.
